rationale: Neither candidate surfaces dsocek's core ground-truth concern (unify naming-conflict handling with _packed_modules_mapping/stacked_params_mapping), but X's suggestion to derive default_components from each pipeline's declared _dit_modules is thematically closer to that critique (eliminate hardcoded/duplicated lists) than Y's shallower 'add a comment noting the registry.py duplication' fix. X also surfaces a genuinely valuable, PR-relevant finding (text-encoder LoRA is still a silent no-op, the same failure class this PR fixes) that outweighs Y's less critical missing-round-trip-test suggestion. Both are well-grounded (they independently cite the same pipeline_sdxl.py:110/46 and registry.py:427 facts, cross-validating accuracy) and both give concrete file/line actionable comments, but X's findings carry more design/severity weight.
